desc:
The caves open up here, going from tighter tunnels to spacious halls. Tall,
black pillars, crudely carved from the surrounding rocks support the vast
ceilings. In some areas, the pillars have been replaced by enormous, old,
withered logs. A long, centrally-located table rises from the floor, hewn
directly from the black stone that forms these caves. It is covered by the
remains of once-living creatures: long-dried entrails, cracked skulls, and
pulverized bones. The vast halls extend to the north and west. To the east a
cracked entranceway leads into a tapering tunnel.
name: Hewn Halls

desc:
The ceilings extend far above, creating halls dozens of feet tall. Pillars of
black stone, bordering on a deep purple, have been carved directly from the
surrounding rock. In some areas, the walls have been reinforced with massive
planks and boulders to prevent a cave-in. The entire area is scattered with
decomposing remains; the smell permeates the humid air. The halls continue to
the east and south.
name: Hewn Halls

desc:
The succession of big, roomy halls appear to end here. A chair of sorts has been
shaped from the stone that forms the northern wall. Its dark stone surface is
covered with dry soil and slimy sediment. The roof is supported by massive, hewn
tree trunks and pillars cut directly from the stone of the mountain. A bonfire
flickers with a fading light at the centre of the cave. The halls continue to
the south.
name: Hewn Halls

desc:
The stairs lead down, deeper into the dark mountain. The cave is crude, with
rough edges protruding from the walls. The unevenly-cut stairs are covered with
dust, rocks and random debris. Little bugs, roaches and spiders climb the walls
along the stairs. The dark, murky tunnel to the east appears passable.
name: Descending Staircase

desc:
A roughly-cut tunnel makes its way through the dark, cold mountain. Nothing
reflects from the poorly dug-out walls. Sharp, pointy edges protrude from all
surfaces. The ground is covered by a thin layer of dust and dirt. Several small
bat skeletons lie in one corner of the cave, and another has been crushed under
falling stones. East and west are staircases, and to the north the tunnel rises
into a slanting burrow.
name: Murky Tunnel

desc:
The ground is even, worn smooth from many decades of use. A shallow gutter has
been worn in the centre of the tunnel. The burrow rises fifteen or so feet tall,
with a ceiling of exposed, sharp, dark stone edges. The pathway runs up and
down, making it a strenuous route of travel. It extends further into darkness to
the east and south.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
A concave trail has been worn in the black stone underfoot, the result of heavy
use over many years. The burrow slopes down from the west and rises again to the
east, creating a small dip right here. To the north the cave opens up into a
deep chasm, the result of the burrow having been mined too deeply. Some twenty
feet across the deep pit is another ledge.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
A roughly-hewn staircase has been dug out of the deep, dark mountain. Its ascent
is steep, and it turns sharply to the northwest. The steps are quite high,
uneven, and worn-down. The exit to the west holds old hinges, set for a door;
the door frame has long since been shattered. At the bottom of the stairs lies a
stone door, broken into many pieces.
name: Stone Stairs

desc:
A ledge of black slate follows the southern wall of the cavern here. To the
north the architects of this cavern have dug far too deep, exposing a crevice
black as night, and with its bottom too far away to be visible. A similar ledge
as this one can be seen on the other side of the chasm, well out of reach to the
north. The burrow makes its way upwards, both to the east and west.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
Black, surprisingly fresh air rushes past your face as you plunge downwards,
deep, deep down towards the unfathomable depths of this broad crack in the
bedrock of the mountain. Were you not preoccupied with falling to your death,
you would notice that the rock walls appear to be of natural origin, with basalt
columns interspersed with clear mountain crystals and tourmalines.
name: Certain Death

desc:
Black, surprisingly fresh air rushes past your face as you plunge downwards,
deep, deep down towards the unfathomable depths of this broad crack in the
bedrock of the mountain. Were you not preoccupied with falling to your death,
you would notice that the rock walls appear to be of natural origin, with basalt
columns interspersed with clear mountain crystals and tourmalines.
name: Certain Death

desc:
A smooth pathway runs through the burrow, rising and falling with regularity.
The path has a concave shape to it, worn down in the middle after many years of
use. The black walls are rough, with sharp edges poking out here and there. A
small gust of air blows through the burrow every now and then.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
A small crack in the burrow's southern wall allows access to a staircase leading
down. The tunnels through the mountain here have low, rough ceilings, and crude
walls. Cockroaches and little bugs dart across the ground, aiming for the safety
of small cracks and holes in the rock. Low, slanting tunnels extend to the north
and west.
name: Confluence of Tunnels

desc:
This rounded tunnel, dug out from the black bedrock, ends southward at a steep
staircase. The tunnel is quite snug and cumbersome to travel. Bat droppings can
be seen here and there. A thin layer of dust covers most of the tunnel. The
accessway continues in a northernly direction.
name: Accessway

desc:
Three tunnels split apart here, with two of them slowly descending to the north
and south. The ceiling and floor slant from west to east, and a troll burrow
takes shape to the north. The black slate of the tunnel floor is solid
underfoot. The area appears to have been dug out solely for the purpose of
transport; and no embellishments or other niceties are present.
name: Accessway

desc:
It is clear this passage through the burrow has been in use for decades, as a
small concave trail has been worn down the middle of the tunnel by creatures
passing through. The cave is black stone, crudely dug out. Fragments from the
ceiling and walls lie scattered along the sides of the tunnel. The pathway
descends below, and rises slightly to the south.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
This narrow, winding tunnel makes its way through the black mountain, twisting
and turning through the bedrock. The ceiling and walls have a rounded shape to
them, and their surfaces are rough and black. The air is damp, and small pearls
of dew have condensed on the walls. Thick layers of bat droppings can be seen on
the ground. The tunnel continues in an easterly direction.
name: Accessway

desc:
A concave trail runs through the centre of the burrow, the hard black rock
underfoot worn down by time and use. The burrow slopes up and down, as if it
were dug without proper guidance. The pathway is quite arduous to traverse; in
addition to the steep slopes, there are rocks and boulders jutting out from the
walls, creating obstacles that nearly block some stretches of the tunnel. The
burrow continues east and west, and to the south abruptly rises and continues
upwards.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
A smooth, black rock ledge runs east to west along the northern wall of the
burrow. The cave complex has been mined too deeply to the south, and drops away
into a giant chasm, the bottom hidden in darkness. Another ledge can be seen to
the south, across the deep pit. Small webs stretch between sharp edges
protruding from the rough wall to the north, and some of the webs appear to have
entangled some of the small bugs that are ever-present in these caves.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
The burrow comes to an end here, in this foul, unpleasant dugout. The black rock
floor is covered with many broken, yellowed bones and dusty old tools. A small
bonfire lights the area, partially illuminating it with a dying, flickering
light. The crudely made burrow inclines slightly towars the east as it heads
deep into the mountain.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
The burrow floor rises sharply here, as if it were passing over an underground
hill. The black slate underfoot has been worn down, creating a concave trail
leading east and west. Heaps of bat guano cover some of the loose rocks
scattered about the area. The burrow walls are rounded, gradually curving
upwards to become the ceiling in an overhead arc.
name: Slanting Burrow

desc:
The dark burrow makes a north-west turn here, coming from the south. A putrid,
stingy smell spreads across the area, with a swift breeze coming in from the
west. The burrow slopes slightly to the west and south. The cave has been
reinforced with thick tree logs to keep the ceiling from caving in. The black
stone walls are cold and dry.
name: Bend in the Slanting Burrow

desc:
A tiny pocket of air has been created in the aftermath of the cave-in. Large
beams of broken wood lie criss-crossed in the small space, leaving enough
leverage and support to keep the ceiling from collapsing further. There are
several troll-like limbs and bodyparts crushed underneath the black, sharp
rocks. The dust is dense in the air, and the lukewarm temperature creates a
perfect atmosphere for mould, larvae, and worms to infest the decaying bodies.
The stench is beyond putrid. A narrow gap through the black mountain rocks can
be seen to the east. A tiny hole lined by razor-sharp rocks opens up into a
large, vast cavern to the south.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
A giant crack opens up here, far inside the mountain. The height of the fissure
indicates the true depth of these caves, and gazing upwards one can see massive
black stalactites hanging down from the high ceiling. Coarse stone, black as
night, forms the surfaces of the walls. The floor is covered by a layer of black
grit, criss-crossed with large footprints. Broken, twisted skeletons, completely
stripped of all soft tissues, lie tossed aside into the corners. The place is
massive, and stretches further east and south. To the north a cave-in has
occurred, leaving no discernible exits.
name: Heart of the Mountain

desc:
An old stone table sits cracked in the centre of the cave. Wiped clean of any
foodstuffs, only mould and fungi cover it now. A skeleton the size of a
man-child lies broken in several pieces in a corner of the cave. It has been
completely picked clean of any soft tissue. Trickles of dirt and pebbles have
landed in piles across the expanse of the cave - clear evidence of recent,
ongoing seismic activities. An enormous cave opens up to the north, and the
cavern also expands further to the east.
name: Ruined Troll-Hole

desc:
A huge black portal, forged from seven massive stone blocks tethered together by
iron bars and rusted bolts, blocks the northern exit from the cave. A row of
four thick, oxidized iron rings hangs from the gate. The cavern itself is both
wide and tall, with sharp edges of black stone protruding from the ceiling and
walls. Shards of shattered stone lie spread across the ground. Remnants of
man-like bodies have been left here, rotting away in the lukewarmth. In the
southeastern corner a tunnel accessway takes a sharp turn, leading further down
into the depths of Ettenmoors.
name: Before a Great Portal

desc:
Rows of hewn shelves line the northern, southern, and western walls. Empty
barrels stand in deep rows against the walls of the black stone chamber. Chests
of various sizes have been placed here in haphazard manners: some are closed,
others have been cracked open and emptied. Shelves of broken clay urns and jars
have been overturned. Everything is in complete disarray, as if an earthquake
had tossed everything up in the air not too long ago.
name: Troll's Treasure Heap
